name: Goblet Squat
basketballBenefit: Builds explosive leg drive for driving to the basket, jumping, and defensive stance endurance.
description: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell at chest height and squat deep while keeping your chest tall. The goblet position teaches perfect squat mechanics.
coachTips: Keep elbows inside your knees at the bottom
Drive through your heels to stand
Chest stays tall — no rounding forward
Control the descent for 2–3 seconds
skillDescription: Channel lower body power into explosive basketball movements.

name: Romanian Deadlift
basketballBenefit: Strengthens the posterior chain for explosive acceleration, rebounding jumps, and injury prevention.
description: Hip-hinge movement with dumbbells or barbell, lowering weights along your thighs while maintaining a flat back.
coachTips: Push your hips back, not down
Keep the bar/weights close to your body
Feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings at the bottom
Squeeze glutes hard at the top
commonMistakes: Bending the knees too much (becomes a squat)
skillDescription: Dominant hamstrings and glutes for elite-level athleticism.

name: Reverse Lunge
basketballBenefit: Trains single-leg strength and balance critical for basketball cuts, drives, and landing safely.
description: Step backward into a lunge, lowering your back knee toward the floor. Safer on the knees than a forward lunge.
coachTips: Keep your front knee directly above your ankle
Stay tall — don't lean forward
Drive through your front heel to return
Add dumbbells for progression
skillDescription: Single-leg stability for dynamic basketball movements.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
basketballBenefit: The ultimate single-leg strength builder — translates directly to explosive cuts, sprints, and lateral movement.
description: Rear foot elevated on a bench or box, front leg does all the work. A brutal but incredibly effective leg exercise.
coachTips: Front foot far enough forward so knee doesn't go past toes
Keep your torso upright
Control the descent slowly
Don't let your front knee cave inward
commonMistakes: Front foot too close to bench
Using too much weight too soon
skillDescription: Unilateral strength that builds elite-level leg power.

name: Hip Thrust
basketballBenefit: Direct glute power for vertical jump height, sprint speed, and explosive directional changes.
description: Shoulders on bench, drive hips up using glute power. The king of glute exercises.
coachTips: Squeeze glutes hard at the top
Chin tucked — don't hyperextend your neck
Feet flat, knees at 90° at the top
Use a barbell or dumbbell for resistance
commonMistakes: Hyperextending the lower back at the top
Feet too close or far from body
skillDescription: Posterior power for vertical dominance on the court.

name: Side Plank
basketballBenefit: Lateral core stability prevents injury during physical contact and improves balance in all basketball positions.
description: Lie on your side, prop up on one forearm, and lift hips to create a straight line from head to feet.
coachTips: Stack your feet or stagger for balance
Don't let hips sag or pike up
Look straight ahead, not at the floor
Breathe steadily through the hold
commonMistakes: Rotating the torso toward the floor
skillDescription: Lateral core armor — protect your body through every contact.

name: Dead Bug
basketballBenefit: Anti-extension core stability protects your spine during explosive movements and improves body control.
description: Lying on your back, extend opposite arm and leg while keeping your lower back pressed to the floor.
coachTips: Press your lower back firmly into the floor the whole time
Move SLOW — this is about control, not speed
Breathe out as you extend the limbs
Never let your low back lift
commonMistakes: Lower back arching off the floor
Arms and legs not fully extended
skillDescription: Deep core strength for unshakeable body control.

name: Copenhagen Plank
basketballBenefit: Most effective exercise for preventing groin injuries — the #1 basketball injury. Also builds lateral stability for defensive slides.
description: Side plank with top leg elevated on a bench. Extremely challenging adductor and core exercise.
coachTips: Start with the easy variation (bottom knee on ground)
Keep your body in a straight line
Don't let your hips rotate
Work up to the full version gradually
commonMistakes: Top leg not aligned with body
Going too hard too soon (groin injury risk)
skillDescription: Elite adductor strength for injury-proof lateral movement.

name: Box Jump
basketballBenefit: Directly trains the explosive fast-twitch power needed for vertical jump, shot-blocking, and rebounding.
description: Explosive jump onto a box or platform. Focus on landing softly and loading for the next jump.
coachTips: Use your arms — big arm swing helps!
Land softly on both feet, absorb impact
Step (not jump) back down to protect your knees
Focus on maximum effort each rep
commonMistakes: Landing with straight legs (crushes your joints)
Jumping down instead of stepping down
Too high a box too early
skillDescription: Explosive jumping power that elevates your game above the rim.

name: Lateral Band Walk
basketballBenefit: Activates the glute med — the key muscle for lateral speed, defensive slides, and knee injury prevention.
description: Band around ankles or knees, walk sideways in an athletic stance. Every step fires the glute med.
coachTips: Stay low in an athletic stance the whole time
Take controlled steps — don't shuffle feet together
Keep toes pointing forward, not out
Feel the burn on the outer hip
commonMistakes: Standing upright instead of athletic stance
Band too high (around thighs = easier, less effective)
skillDescription: Hip power for elite defensive slides and quick lateral cuts.

name: Pallof Press
basketballBenefit: Anti-rotation core strength for fighting through screens, posting up, and maintaining position under physical pressure.
description: Hold a cable or band at chest height, press straight out and resist the rotation force. The best anti-rotation exercise.
coachTips: Stand perpendicular to the anchor point
Press straight out — don't twist
Hold for 2 seconds at full extension
Keep hips level and stable
skillDescription: Anti-rotation strength for holding your ground in physical play.

name: Turkish Get-Up
basketballBenefit: The ultimate full-body coordination exercise — builds shoulder stability, core control, and total-body movement quality.
description: Starting lying down with a kettlebell locked overhead, stand up while keeping the weight locked overhead the entire time. One of the most complex human movements.
coachTips: Start with no weight — nail the movement pattern first
Eyes on the bell the ENTIRE time
Every transition is slow and controlled
Think of it as 7 individual exercises in one
commonMistakes: Not learning each step individually first
skillDescription: LEGENDARY — Master of total body coordination and control.

name: Jump Rope
basketballBenefit: Improves footwork, coordination, and cardiovascular conditioning — directly translates to on-court agility.
description: Classic jump rope — single bounces, staying on the balls of your feet. Build endurance and foot speed simultaneously.
coachTips: Stay on the balls of your feet — never flat-footed
Small jumps — just enough clearance for the rope
Elbows stay close to your body
Land softly to protect your joints
skillDescription: Lightning foot speed and court agility conditioning.

name: Bike Sprint
basketballBenefit: High-intensity intervals build the explosive cardiovascular capacity needed for full-court sprints and pressing defense.
description: Short, maximal effort sprints on a stationary bike (or outdoor). 20 seconds all-out, 40 seconds easy.
coachTips: All-out means ALL OUT on the sprint — 100% effort
Keep resistance high enough to prevent spinning
Active recovery during rest (keep pedaling slow)
Count: 5 rounds minimum
commonMistakes: Not going hard enough on sprints
skillDescription: Unstoppable cardiovascular capacity for 40 hard minutes.

name: Hip Flexor Stretch
basketballBenefit: Tight hip flexors limit your stride length and vertical jump. Releasing them unlocks explosive athletic performance.
description: Kneeling lunge stretch with weight shifted forward, feeling a deep stretch through the front of the hip.
coachTips: Squeeze your back glute to deepen the stretch
Keep your torso tall
Breathe deeply into the stretch
Hold 45–60 seconds each side
skillDescription: Unlock your full athletic range of motion on the court.

name: Foam Roll — Quads
basketballBenefit: Regular quad rolling prevents the knee pain that ends basketball careers. Essential maintenance for heavy leg training days.
description: Lie face down with foam roller under your quads. Slowly roll from hip to knee, pausing on tight spots.
coachTips: Roll slowly — 1 inch per second
Pause on tight spots for 10–20 seconds
Rotate your leg in/out to hit different angles
Don't roll over joints (knees, hips)
commonMistakes: Not enough body weight on the roller
skillDescription: Tissue quality maintenance for long-term athletic longevity.
